# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any
    parameters {
        string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: '', description: 'Docker image tag')
        string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: '', description: 'Docker registry URL')
    }
    environment {
        DOCKER_CREDENTIALS_ID = 'docker-credentials' // Jenkins credentials ID for Docker
    }
    stages {
        stage('Validate Parameters') {
            steps {
                script {
                    if (!params.IMAGE_TAG || !params.REGISTRY_URL) {
                        error('IMAGE_TAG and REGISTRY_URL parameters must be provided.')
                    }
                    if (!params.IMAGE_TAG.matches('^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$')) {
                        error('Invalid IMAGE_TAG format.')
                    }
                    if (!params.REGISTRY_URL.matches('^https?://[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+(:[0-9]+)?(/.*)?$')) {
                        error('Invalid REGISTRY_URL format.')
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    docker.withRegistry(params.REGISTRY_URL, DOCKER_CREDENTIALS_ID) {
                        def image = docker.build("${params.REGISTRY_URL}/myapp:${params.IMAGE_TAG}")
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        stage('Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    docker.withRegistry(params.REGISTRY_URL, DOCKER_CREDENTIALS_ID) {
                        def image = docker.image("${params.REGISTRY_URL}/myapp:${params.IMAGE_TAG}")
                        image.push()
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    post {
        always {
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
